school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: The class with Iris' period is between Carver's period and the class with 80%'s period.
Exactly one of these is true: Boyd goes with 84%; Dunn goes with 78%; Dunn goes with Period 5.
82% goes with exactly two of Hale, Piper, and Period 2.
Between Dunn and Kira, one goes with Period 1 and the other with Period 3.
Iris goes with either Nash or 84% (but not both).
Period 5 goes with exactly two of Carver, Liam, and 76%.
Either Kira goes with Frost or Period 1 goes with Dunn if and only if either 82% does not go with Iris or Carver does not go with Period 2 (but not both).
Between Boyd and Noah, one goes with 78% and the other with Period 3.
Either 82% goes with Hale or Hale goes with Liam.
Exactly one of these is true: Boyd goes with Liam; Carver goes with Piper; Noah goes with 84%.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: The Benoni player's placing is between Garry's placing and the 1650-rated player's placing.
Exactly one of these is true: Boris goes with 1750; Judit goes with 1600; Judit goes with 2nd.
1700 goes with exactly two of Nadia, Sicilian, and 5th.
Between Judit and Italian, one goes with 6th and the other with 4th.
Benoni goes with either Vera or 1750 (but not both).
2nd goes with exactly two of Garry, London, and 1550.
Either Italian goes with Mikhail or 6th goes with Judit if and only if either 1700 does not go with Benoni or Garry does not go with 5th (but not both).
Between Boris and Pirc, one goes with 1600 and the other with 4th.
Either 1700 goes with Nadia or Nadia goes with London.
Exactly one of these is true: Boris goes with London; Garry goes with Sicilian; Pirc goes with 1750.
